stemHtml: Which of the following is a potential risk of carrying supplemental oxygen canisters onboard?

options.a: When mixed with oil or grease, spontaneous combustion could happen.
options.b: At high altitude and low outside pressure, the pressure of the oxygen system might not be enough for normal respiration.
options.c: After maintenance there is always some residual grease or oil left, and mixing it with oxygen could result in toxic gases.
options.d: Pure oxygen in cannisters runs out too fast, and will not last until the aircraft gets to a safe altitude.
